characters.name: Antoinette
characters.description: AntoinettewasDoctor Miguelito Loveless' main subordinate, confidant and lover, being the only person Loveless ever treated with compassion and spared from his heinous cruelty. James Westfirst encountered Antoinette after being forcefully escorted to Loveless' lair after finding out about his explosive schemes, where he witness her playing a harpsichord and later singing a duet with Loveless to entertain West and Lundquist. After Loveless's plans are thwarted and he is placed in prison, Antoinette is not seen again, presumably having eluded capture. However this would not be the last of them as Dr. Loveless later escaped prison along with his henchman Voltaire and reunited with Antoinette where they hatched a new scheme to capture West and use him for their plans. Later on, West is drugged byMarie Pincher, and taken to a town filled with mannequins with piped in sounds. This time Loveless has developed a plan to kidnap Jim and replace him with an exact double, Janus. With Janus, Loveless will destroy the Secret Service, get his explosive formula back. Finally, he will blackmail the U.S. into handing over Southern California, which he still says should inherently be his. Loveless has invented plastic surgery, and supposedly, he is far better at it than our modern surgeons have become. When West wakes up in on the operating table, Loveless is dressed in a lab coat, and Antoinette is once again playing on the harpsichord. When the time for the medical transfer is set to take place, both Antoinette and Marie assist in nurses outfits. Artie helps rescue Jim and foil Loveless' plot. West blows the circuits, which in turn destroys the lab. A fist fight ensues and West is able to knock Janus out. Voltaire was electrocuted, but survived. He and Loveless were both sent back to prison. Marie escaped prosecution and was last seen back at West's caboose giving him a kiss. There is no mention of whether Antoinette was sent to prison or if she eluded capture.

characters.name: Artemus Gordon
characters.description: Real Name:Artemus Gordon Other Aliases:Arte, Aaron Addison, Angus MacGordon, Ascot Sam, Beldon Scoville, Jr., Captain Hull, Caruso del Artumo, Claude Renard, Colonel Cross, Colonel Doyle, Colonel Hernandez, Colonel Mosley, Count Draja, Count Felix Von Schleiswieg, Count Vladislov de Raja, Dirk de John, Dr. Crane, Dr. Gentry, Dr. Raker, Dr. Romonede Pettriere, Dr. Sloane, Dr. Zorbe, Ellsworth R. Caldwell, President Grant, Mojave Mike and on and on and on and on and on and on and on. Marital Status:Single (see more below)

characters.name: Artemus Gordon
characters.description: Real Name:Artemus Gordon Other Aliases:Arte, Aaron Addison, Angus MacGordon, Ascot Sam, Beldon Scoville, Jr., Captain Hull, Caruso del Artumo, Claude Renard, Colonel Cross, Colonel Doyle, Colonel Hernandez, Colonel Mosley, Count Draja, Count Felix Von Schleiswieg, Count Vladislov de Raja, Dirk de John, Dr. Crane, Dr. Gentry, Dr. Raker, Dr. Romonede Pettriere, Dr. Sloane, Dr. Zorbe, Ellsworth R. Caldwell, President Grant, Mojave Mike and on and on and on and on and on and on and on. Marital Status:Single (see more below)

characters.name: Belladonna
characters.description: Belladonnais a gun moll who becameDoctor Miguelito Loveless's second consort some time afterAntoinettedisappearance. Unlike Antoinette, Loveless held no true love or loyalty for Belladonna, only keeping her around to admire her beauty, as with most of his consorts after the loss of Antoinette. Dr. Loveless (Michael Dunn) is going to try to take over the western part of the United States.  This time he is training an army of his own to perform the take over. He acts like a stage director with his goons practicing their roles in a model of a bank. Belladonna is posed as a old lady making a deposit with a bank teller. It goes wrong, and the man playing James West is shot and the bullet ended up real. The man responsible for not changing his bullets to blanks is shot by another on orders of Loveless. The next day they successfully rob a bank and then burn the money in the desert. They do this, because it is all chicken scratch, and the fact that they know the banknotes all have serial numbers on them that can be traced back.

characters.name: Braine
characters.description: Brainewas a megalomaniacal and tactical evil genius who sought to replace all the major world leaders with his own subordinates in order to ravage the world through war and then rule over what remained. A man fascinated with strategy, probability, tactics and most of all, chess, Braine believes in total conquest and enjoys predicting every move his enemies will make, and because of this he treats everything like a game of chess.

characters.name: Braine
characters.description: Brainewas a megalomaniacal and tactical evil genius who sought to replace all the major world leaders with his own subordinates in order to ravage the world through war and then rule over what remained. A man fascinated with strategy, probability, tactics and most of all, chess, Braine believes in total conquest and enjoys predicting every move his enemies will make, and because of this he treats everything like a game of chess.

characters.name: Carmelita Loveless
characters.description: Carmelita Lovelesswas the daughter of the infamous criminal geniusDoctor Miguelito Lovelessand the sister of the even more dangerousDoctor Miguelito Loveless, Jr.. After the death of their father, Carmelita aided her brother in running their father's criminal empire, with Carmelita mainly focusing on secretly managing the town ofWagon Gapwhich served as a cover for their criminal affairs as well as the location of their civilian home and business, with the town even being connected to their secret underground headquarters.

characters.name: Carmelita Loveless
characters.description: Carmelita Lovelesswas the daughter of the infamous criminal geniusDoctor Miguelito Lovelessand the sister of the even more dangerousDoctor Miguelito Loveless, Jr.. After the death of their father, Carmelita aided her brother in running their father's criminal empire, with Carmelita mainly focusing on secretly managing the town ofWagon Gapwhich served as a cover for their criminal affairs as well as the location of their civilian home and business, with the town even being connected to their secret underground headquarters.

characters.name: Caroline Day
characters.description: Caroline Daywas the mother ofListon Lawrence Day, a soldier of the Texas Rebellion thirty years ago who was arrested for seemingly betraying the USA and causing the death of countless soldiers, and after her son's arrest she died alone and miserable, but the love for her son continued to linger in the living world, mysteriously transcending time and became one with her family's manor, transforming her into a "living" haunted mansion where the normal flow of time was null and void, and where her essence continued to linger, longing for the day that her son could return home.

characters.name: Caroline Day
characters.description: Caroline Daywas the mother ofListon Lawrence Day, a soldier of the Texas Rebellion thirty years ago who was arrested for seemingly betraying the USA and causing the death of countless soldiers, and after her son's arrest she died alone and miserable, but the love for her son continued to linger in the living world, mysteriously transcending time and became one with her family's manor, transforming her into a "living" haunted mansion where the normal flow of time was null and void, and where her essence continued to linger, longing for the day that her son could return home.

characters.name: Colonel Richmond
characters.description: Colonel Richmondwas the head of the US Secret Service. He served asJames WestandArtemus Gordon's superior officer. However as the two were his top agents who worked directly underPresident Grant, he often treated them with the highest admiration and reverence.

characters.name: Count Carlos Manzeppi
characters.description: Count Carlos Maria Vincenzo Robespierre Manzeppiwas a mysterious assassin and insane megalomaniac who was a man of almost unparalleled genius as well as a master of the occult, who served as the leader ofThe Eccentrics, a guild of assassins renowned for their eccentric behavior as well their frighteningly demented method of operation. Manzeppi himself wasJames WestandArtemus Gordon's other major recurring foe, in a similar manner to West and Gordon's arch-enemyDoctor Miguelito Loveless, however unlike Loveless, Manzeppi only faced the duo twice before disappearing, and in regards to his character, while Loveless was a mentally disturbed victim of society whose actions became more evil and demented as time went on, Manzeppi was nothing short of evil incarnate from the start, possibly even of demonic origin himself, with abilities that defy all explanation, being able to phase through walls, disappear at will or even manipulate fire. Aside from these abilities, he is also a genius with scientific talents that rival Loveless himself, who is decades ahead of his time, with Manzeppi being able to harness electricity, create disintegration chambers, security cameras, video transmitters, human-like robots and develop complex weapons and explosives that rival or even surpass those of the Secret Service, but are always in the form of something amusing or juvenile. Manzeppi's greatest feat however was discovering the legendary Philosopher's Stone itself after traversing through ancient ruins, the darkest pits and places where blood flowed freely.Manzeppi's foolish and greedy assistant stole the Philosopher's Stone from him and was turned into gold and then dust! Manzeppi does not retrive the Philosopher's Stone after realizing even he cannot control it!

Manzeppi does not retrive the Philosopher's Stone after realizing even he cannot control it!

characters.name: Delilah
characters.description: Delilahwas a mistress of many disguises andDoctor Miguelito Loveless's fourth and last known consort after abandoningTristeandBelladonna, and losingAntoinette. Unlike Antoinette, but much like with Triste and Belladonna, Loveless held no real love or loyalty for Triste, keeping her around only for her beauty. When we first see Delilah, she is posing as a lady barber in a shop that James T. West (Robert Conrad) goes in to get a shave. After she covers his face with a hot towel, the man sitting next to him is revealed to be Dr. Miguelito Loveless (Michael Dunn), who quickly goes over to the unsuspecting West and pours a knockout liquid through the opening. West wakes up in a peculiar circus show, where he sees several strange signs and the local judge tied up. After fighting with several hired thugs, West is knocked out. When he awakens, he is sitting in the barber chair and Delilah is now a much older lady.

characters.name: Delilah
characters.description: Delilahwas a mistress of many disguises andDoctor Miguelito Loveless's fourth and last known consort after abandoningTristeandBelladonna, and losingAntoinette. West wakes up in a peculiar circus show, where he sees several strange signs and the local judge tied up. After fighting with several hired thugs, West is knocked out. When he awakens, he is sitting in the barber chair and Delilah is now a much older lady.

characters.name: Doctor Miguelito Loveless
characters.description: Dr. Miguelito Quixote Lovelessis a brilliant and insane dwarf who serves as the arch-enemy of agentsJames WestandArtemus Gordon. A mad scientist whose intellect is hundreds of years ahead of his time, Dr. Loveless was involved in numerous plots that threatened the USA and even the whole world which were always foiled by West and Gordon, but he always managed to elude capture. His original goal was the conquest of California, which he claimed was his birthright as a Spanish nobleman. Interesting fact about Michael Dunn who played Dr loveless, and Robert Conrad who played James west. When the script called for an appearance from Dr loveless, actor Michael Dunn would stay over at Robert Conrad's home has his personal guest. He was known as a technological genius, creating scientific marvels that were not only centuries ahead of their time but could even pierce the boundaries of reality. Loveless initially had two constant companions, the giganticVoltaire, (Played by actor Richard Keil) and the beautiful songstressAntoinette.

characters.name: Doctor Miguelito Loveless, Jr.
characters.description: Doctor Miguelito Loveless, Jr.was the son of the infamous criminal geniusDoctor Miguelito Lovelessand the inheritor of his criminal empire and legacy which he oversaw alongside his sisterCarmelita Lovelessafter his father's death. Much like his father before him, Loveless Jr. was an evil genius whose intellect and creativity was not only centuries ahead of his time but was even further ahead than his father, having successfully created bionic limbs, cloning technology and even recreating his father's lost formula for an atomic bomb.  You can see the TV Series on Youtube here:Old West Theater on YouTube has Wild Wild West series and movies.

characters.name: Doctor Miguelito Loveless, Jr.
characters.description: Doctor Miguelito Loveless, Jr.was the son of the infamous criminal geniusDoctor Miguelito Lovelessand the inheritor of his criminal empire and legacy which he oversaw alongside his sisterCarmelita Lovelessafter his father's death. Much like his father before him, Loveless Jr. was an evil genius whose intellect and creativity was not only centuries ahead of his time but was even further ahead than his father, having successfully created bionic limbs, cloning technology and even recreating his father's lost formula for an atomic bomb. characters.name: Frank Harper
characters.description: Frank Harperwas a Secret Service agent for the US who was briefly paired up withJames WestafterArtemus Gordonwas briefly indisposed during the line of duty.

characters.name: James West
characters.description: James T. West(Born July 2, 1842) was a special agent in the US Secret Service who served directly underPresident Grantand was considered the best agent the government had to offer alongside his loyal partner and best friendArtemus Gordon. West was born in the US on July 2, 1842 (many events, however, have contradictory dates unless we assume that the stories are not told in chronological order, which is entirely possible since they are almost completely episodic). Little is known of the early history and childhood of James T. West. In fact, it is not even known what the "T" in his name stands for. He is known to have mentioned his father once but there was not enough information to draw any knowledge about the man. What is known is that Jim West graduated from West Point college where he was mentored by archaeology expertProfessor Robey. After graduating from West Point, Jim subsequently served as a Cavalry officer, rising to the rank of Major in the United States Army. He was decorated many times. He then served in the Union Army during the Civil War, attaining the rank of Captain. In 1865, when he was a lieutenant, near Vicksburg, Virginia, in the aftermath of a battle, he stumbled upon a wounded Confederate soldier. The man’s legs had been severely damaged. West applied tourniquets that stopped the bleeding and saved the man’s life, a man by the name ofNoel Bartley Vautrain. This particular incident would later have severe repercussions in West's career as a Secret Service agent. James West is a handsome ladies man. Although small in stature, he is a coiled spring of energy. He usually wears a blue western style suit complete with vest, cowboy hat and numerous concealed weapons and devices. A martial arts master, West’s speed is amazing. Regularly defeating groups of at least four opponents in hand-to-hand fights, he was equally unmatched with guns, once defeating even an android in a fast-draw contest. Unlike his partner Artemus Gordon, Jim relies on his fists to get him past his enemies, or his seductive wiles to charm the ladies. He also has a knack for safe cracking which he picked up along the way in his travels. He travels in style aboard the Wanderer, a specially-equipped private train with richly appointed compartments containing a wide array of special of his suit coat, a breakaway two-shot derringer he can assemble from pieces contained in his bootheels, a grappling hook and line that can be fired from his revolver, and a special ring that can cut through iron bars. On occasion, he supplements this gear with more specialized equipment.

characters.name: Jeremy Pike
characters.description: Jeremy Pikewas a Secret Service agent for the US who was briefly paired up withJames WestafterArtemus Gordonwas briefly indisposed during the line of duty.

characters.name: Marie Pincher
characters.description: Marie Pincherwas an accomplice ofDoctor Miguelito Lovelesswho had been recruited by him after his third escape from prison along with his henchmanVoltaire. When they first see her,James WestandArtemus Gordonare attracted to the beautiful blonde sitting at a table by herself. Artie strikes out, but when the two exit the restaurant, the girl drops her fan and Jim is obliged to pick it up. They learn the she is Marie Pincher, and West leaves the establishment with her. Once inside the carriage a gas is released, and West is knocked out. Fortunately Marie has a gas mask available for her to avoid the poison. When West regains consciousness he finds himself in a town filled with mannequins with piped in sounds. This time Loveless has developed a plan to kidnap Jim and replace him with an exact double,Janus. With Janus, Loveless will destroy the Secret Service, get his explosive formula back. Finally, he will blackmail the U.S. into handing over Southern California, which he still says should inherently be his.

characters.name: Marie Pincher
characters.description: Marie Pincherwas an accomplice ofDoctor Miguelito Lovelesswho had been recruited by him after his third escape from prison along with his henchmanVoltaire. Once inside the carriage a gas is released, and West is knocked out. Fortunately Marie has a gas mask available for her to avoid the poison. When West regains consciousness he finds himself in a town filled with mannequins with piped in sounds. This time Loveless has developed a plan to kidnap Jim and replace him with an exact double,Janus. With Janus, Loveless will destroy the Secret Service, get his explosive formula back. Finally, he will blackmail the U.S. into handing over Southern California, which he still says should inherently be his.

characters.name: Morn, Pan and Alna
characters.description: Morn,PanandAlnaare three beautiful young alien women who supposedly crash landed on Earth. In truth though, they are really a trio of vaudeville players known as theCourtney Sisters, hired as larcenous henchwomen to aid the villainous Ben Victor in his scheme to fleece a town of all its government gold. Morn (real name,Maggie) is the main spokeswoman for the "aliens". Falling for the charms of agentJames West, to an extent she tries to help him avoid danger. These three "alien" women emerge from a supposed UFO which has been "forced to land" on the outskirts of a western town. They claim to be travelers from Venus, though are actually part of an audacious plot to steal the federal government gold being kept temporarily in the town, guarded by agents James West and Artemus Gordon, as well as some soldiers. Morn decoys Jim at a picnic, while in town the other girls are busy knocking out Arte and the guards with their "outer space" gas guns and helping male villain Ben Victor take the government gold from the safe - carrying it away in a wagon along with a kidnapped Artemus Gordon. After also taking Jim prisoner, the villains plan to blow the 2 federal agents up in the phony UFO. In the climax of the episode after the male villains are vanquished, Arte gets himself into a wild comical scuffle with the criminal girls in the covered wagon. Morn climbs into the front to join Jim at the reigns and starts to get romantic with him; West matter of factly says to her that all the girls are all going to jail. Later in their special train Jim gets a telegraph message that Pan and Alna have been given 5 - 10 years in jail and Morn 1 year (presumably a lesser sentence because she had, to an extent, tried to help West out).

characters.name: Noel Bartley Vautrain
characters.description: Noel Bartley Vautrainwas a former Confederate Army colonel and a powerful esper with mental powers capable of altering time and space itself. Because of his abilities, he could potentially rewrite history and temporarily remove anything he desired from existence, making him one ofJames WestandArtemus Gordon's most dangerous enemies. He was also the uncle ofAmanda Vautrain. Vautrain was born to the wealthy Vautrain family in the South and became the head of his family's estate. When the Civil War began, he served as a Confederate colonel. During the final days of the war, Vautrain's legs were badly wounded and was on Death's doorstep. Luckily, he survived after a mercifulJames Westof the Union Army saved his life all those years ago by applying first aid, but unfortunately the damage to his legs was so severe that they were amputated, resulting in him being forever bound to a wheelchair, an existence he would grow to despise tremendously as the years passed as he was completely confined to his own manor with no freedom to go anywhere, living his life in a decaying prison which made him long for death, and which resulted in him despising his savior, James West, seeing him as the one who doomed him to a life of personal imprisonment. His only company being his niece Amanda, who sadly became the victim of her uncle's anger and resentment. As the years passed, Vautrain had little to do with his confined existence, except practice magic trick and read books on all manner of subjects, from mystical phenomenon to metaphysical theory. It was during these years that through his hatred, he discovered a mysterious ability he had never known before, seemingly being able to make things disappear if he concentrated his mind and emotions on them. This fascinated him and resulted him in researching more about this mysterious phenomenon, realizing that he was actually accessing the 4th Dimension, a realm beyond time and space where he could not only send things away to a different place in space but even move objects through time. It was then that he theorized that through his new ascended state that he could potentially alter the past as well and thus save his legs.

characters.name: President Grant
characters.description: Ulysses S. Grantwas the president of the United States of America from 1869 to 1877. As the head of state, President Grant oversaw all branches of the US government, and most importantly the US Secret Service, with his two top agents beingJames WestandArtemus Gordon, both of whom were one of the few to answer directly to the president and take their orders directly from him. Whenever he appeared before West and Gordon, his life was usually at risk and it was up to his two top agents to save his life and maintain the security and stability of the US. Grant, West and Gordon have been working together since the Civil War, where West in particular served as one of Grant's top captains in the Union Army. Although Grant probably never knew it, West and Gordon had also saved his life in the past and secured the victory for the Union Army after they were sent back in time by former confederate colonel,Noel Bartley Vautrain, whose nature as an esper allowed him to access the 4th dimension and alter the past, where they stopped Vautrain from killing Grant in an ambush.

characters.name: Tennyson
characters.description: Tennysonwas a servant briefly under the employ ofJames WestandArtemus Gordonwho briefly served as a chauffeur and butler for the duo when needed, while also loosely helping them on their missions.

characters.name: Triste
characters.description: Tristewas a murderous woman andDoctor Miguelito Loveless's third consort after abandoningBelladonnaand losingAntoinette. Unlike Antoinette, but much like with Belladonna, Loveless held no real love or loyalty for Triste, keeping her around only for her beauty, but despite this, Triste did genuinely care about Loveless even after he abandoned her. At the beginning of the episode Dr. Miguelito Loveless (Michael Dunn) has faked his own death. This was the ninth of ten episodes that Loveless appeared in the Wild Wild West series. James T. West (Robert Conrad) and Artemus Gordon have been called in to identify his body at the local morgue.  While there, a mysterious woman shows up to mourn, and she takes a key from the wrist of the supposed deceased. She does it in a way that she is assured that West would notice, and immediately pulled a gun on West. When the mortician went for his gun, she shot and killed him. However, in the commotion, West was able to take her gun away. One of Loveless' enemies came in and a skirmish broke out, which resulted in a fire starting in the parlor. The key leads them to a bank safe deposit box, which blows up when they attempt to open it. Luckily, they survive the trap, and locate a red whistle and a letter which sends them on a wild goose chase.

characters.name: Voltaire
characters.description: Voltairewas a hulking giant of a man who served asDoctor Miguelito Loveless' main muscleman and servant. Little was known of Voltaire, especially because of his lack of speech, however Voltaire had been in the service of Loveless for quite some time. His abilities were nothing short of superhuman, capable of crushing metal with ease, being immune to ordinary hand-to-hand combat, and even being capable of surviving electric bolts strong enough to take down an elephant with only minor burns. The reasons for these superhuman feats are never clarified but it appears to be Loveless' doing, asArtemus Gordononce noticed that Voltaire was somehow growing bigger and even gained the ability to talk, apparently gifts from Dr. Loveless, himself. Voltaire was also implied to have received his name from Loveless and Loveless andAntoinettewere even shown to be teaching him to read at a basic level before he learned to speak. Due to Loveless' near-limitless genius, it was possible that Voltaire was an artificial human created by Loveless.
